name: Italian Nougat
recipeInstructions:
THIS CANDY IS USUALLY ONLY MADE DURING THE EASTER HOLIDAYS
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Spread filberts and almonds on a baking sheet. Toast in the oven for 10 minutes, until golden.
2. In a heavy, straight-sided saucepan, combine sugar, corn syrup, honey, salt, and 1/4 cup water. Stir over medium heat until the sugar is dissolved. Continue cooking without stirring to 252°F on a candy thermometer, or until a small amount in cold water forms a hard ball.
3. In a large bowl of an electric mixer, beat egg whites at high speed until stiff peaks form.
4. In a thin stream, pour about 1/4 of the hot syrup over the egg whites, beating constantly with high speed for 5 minutes until the mixture is stiff enough to hold its shape. Cook the remaining syrup to 315°F-318°F on a candy thermometer, or until a small amount in cold water forms brittle threads.
5. In a thin stream, pour the hot syrup over the meringue, beating constantly with high speed until stiff enough to hold its shape. Add vanilla and butter, beating until thickened again—about 5 minutes. Stir in toasted nuts.
6. Turn mixture into a buttered 11x7x1-1/4 inch pan. Smooth the top with a spatula. Refrigerate until firm.
7. Loosen the edge of the candy all around; turn out onto a large cutting board. With a sharp knife, cut into 1-1/2x1-inch pieces.
8. Wrap each piece in waxed paper. Refrigerate.
Yield: Makes 2-1/2 pounds
